title: White House Shrugs Off Shaky Economy as War Exceeds Trump’s Timeline
summary: Stocks may be soaring again, but the war in Iran has started to pinch the finances of many Americans.

title: Jesus Memes, Threats and a War in Iran: A Portrait of Trump Under Pressure
summary: President Trump has lashed out at enemies, allies and even the pope, and made it harder for Republicans to keep the focus on economic issues in a midterm election year.

title: RFK Jr. to Reform Health Panel That Determines Which Screenings Insurers Cover
summary: The health secretary said he would reform the panel, which determines whether insurance will cover preventive services like mammograms and colonoscopies.

title: Yale Report Finds Colleges Deserve Blame for Higher Education’s Problems
summary: A 10-member committee offered a brutal assessment of academia’s role in creating the forces challenging American colleges and universities.

title: Mounting Troubles at Ohio State University Lead to Frustration
summary: Ohio State isn’t the only university in turmoil, but few others have faced so many issues lately. One lawmaker called the school “a national embarrassment.”

title: ICE Agent Charged With Assault in Rare State Prosecution
summary: Minnesota prosecutors have spent weeks investigating the conduct of immigration agents who took part in an immigration crackdown in the Twin Cities.

title: ICE Has Detained Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé, an 85-Year-Old Widow
summary: After Marie-Thérèse Ross-Mahé’s husband died, an inheritance battle exploded. Her stepson then used his influence to have her arrested, an Alabama probate judge said.

title: The Costa Rican Mountain Town Offering Sanctuary to Families Deported by Trump
summary: In a cloud forest village, a network of residents, foreigners and pacifist Quakers offered a precarious yet vital sanctuary for families expelled by the U.S. government.

title: Venezuelan Doctor Ezequiel Veliz in Texas Is Released From Detention
summary: Dr. Ezequiel Veliz, a family physician who worked in an area that has a doctor shortage, was detained for 10 days. Another Venezuelan doctor remains locked up.

title: Artemis II Crew to Discuss NASA Moon Mission and Next Steps
summary: The four astronauts, who last spoke to the public on Sunday, will speak at a news conference Thursday afternoon at Johnson Space Center in Houston.

title: Judge Again Halts Aboveground Construction on Trump Ballroom
summary: A federal judge wrote that an exception he made for work on security features did not cover most of the construction on the larger ballroom project Trump has proposed.

title: Virginia Ex-Lt. Gov. Justin Fairfax Kills Wife and Self, Police Say
summary: Mr. Fairfax, a Democrat, served as lieutenant governor from 2018 to 2022. The couple’s children were home at the time, the police said, and their son called 911.

title: Senate Votes to Allow Mining Near Boundary Waters Wilderness
summary: The move was a victory for a Chilean company that wants to build a copper and nickel mine, which environmentalists say could devastate fragile lakes and forests.

title: Court Rejects Trump Administration Climate Lawsuit Against Hawaii
summary: In a setback for federal efforts to thwart climate litigation, the judge ruled that the suit, which tried to block the state from suing oil companies, was too speculative.

title: Can Webcams Help Solve New York’s Restaurant Line Problem?
summary: With carefully positioned cameras and user input, the website Damn Lines hopes to address the worst part of visiting popular restaurants.

title: Where Has All of New York City’s Outdoor Dining Gone?
summary: The number of eateries with permits for sidewalk and roadway tables has dwindled to about a third of its pandemic-era peak.

title: Kendrick Lamar’s Protégé Baby Keem Tells the Whole Story, Warts and All
summary: The 25-year-old rapper and producer knows he’s benefited from his cousin’s support. But the path to his autobiographical album, “Casino,” was his alone.

title: Utah Becomes the New Center of U.S. Measles Cases
summary: Nearly 600 people have been sickened across the state, which has seen an increase in vaccine exemptions among children in recent years.

title: Batch of Anti-Anxiety Drug Xanax Recalled, F.D.A. Says
summary: The recall affects only a small number of pills, sold nationwide under the brand name Xanax XR.

title: Luke Tennie’s Journey From ‘Shrinking’ to ‘The Pitt’ and Beyond
summary: After a breakout performance on “Shrinking,” the actor can now be seen on “Abbott Elementary” and “The Pitt.”

title: If You Love ‘The Pitt,’ You’ll Love These Memoirs by Real E.R. Doctors
summary: Memoirs from the front lines capture the high-octane pace, roller coaster stakes and unforgettable personalities of emergency medicine.

title: How the Supreme Court Defeated Trump
summary: A conservative court watcher explains why the president has failed to bend the judicial branch to his will.

title: Older Women Are in Demand by Younger Men
summary: What a shift in the dating preferences of younger men reveals about our changing norms.

title: National Security. Artificial Intelligence. And Your Dumb Dog.
summary: We’re covering today’s top stories.

title: Former N.B.A. Player Is Expected to Plead Guilty in Gambling Scandal
summary: Damon Jones, who spent more than a decade in the N.B.A., was accused of providing inside information about injured players to professional gamblers.

title: Russian Strikes Kill at Least 15 in Ukraine in Biggest Attack in Months
summary: Moscow is again ramping up missile and drone attacks on civilian targets, dispelling any notion that a temporary cease-fire for Orthodox Easter might become more lasting.

title: A Baker, Cardamom Buns and Allegations of a $3.8 Million Scam
summary: A baker at the Church of Sweden’s popular cafe in Midtown Manhattan was accused of swindling nearly $4 million from the church’s donations and bequests.
